primary_accounting_issue: Détermination du critère « solely payments of principal and interest » (SPPI) des clauses contractuelles d’un actif financier selon IFRS 9, comme condition de sa classification ultérieure.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I selected IFRS 9 because it directly governs the contractual cash flow characteristics test for financial assets, including whether cash flows are solely payments of principal and interest; no overlapping higher-precedence governing document in the context addresses that same issue.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low — the question is squarely about the SPPI test, which is expressly addressed in IFRS 9 paragraphs 4.1.2, 4.1.2A, 4.1.3 and B4.1.7–B4.1.26.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly define the SPPI test and link it to the possible classification models for financial assets.

supporting_authority.reason: This supports that IFRS 9 classification is the relevant model when financial assets are classified at acquisition, but it does not itself govern the SPPI test.

peripheral_authority.reason: IAS 32 helps distinguish financial instruments generally, but it does not govern the SPPI classification criterion for financial assets.
IFRIC 12 addresses service concession receivables and points to IFRS 9, but it does not add peer approaches for the SPPI issue itself.
IAS 19 mentions financial instruments and plan assets in other contexts, which are not relevant to identifying SPPI-based classification approaches.

treatment_families.family: Financial assets with contractual cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest and held to collect contractual cash flows
Financial assets with contractual cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest and held both to collect contractual cash flows and to sell financial assets
Financial assets that fail the SPPI condition or are otherwise not within an amortised cost or fair value through other comprehensive income business model

approaches.label: Amortised cost
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 requires this measurement basis when the asset has SPPI cash flows and is held in a business model to collect contractual cash flows.

approaches.label: Fair value through other comprehensive income
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 requires this measurement basis when the asset has SPPI cash flows and is held in a business model achieved by both collecting contractual cash flows and selling financial assets.

approaches.label: Fair value through profit or loss
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 uses this as the classification outcome for financial assets that do not meet the SPPI condition or do not fit the qualifying business models for the other two approaches.
